task_completion.description: Open the dataset in Jamovi and set up the One-Way ANOVA variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi application is open with the 'Viagra' dataset loaded. The 'One-Way ANOVA' analysis panel is active, with 'libido' placed in the 'Dependent Variables' box and 'dose' placed in the 'Fixed Factors' box.

task_completion.description: Set the contrast for the dose factor to Polynomial.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jamovi ANOVA options panel, under the 'Contrasts' section, the drop-down or selection for the 'dose' factor is set to 'Polynomial'.

task_completion.description: Enable Homogeneity test and Omega-squared effect size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jamovi ANOVA options panel, the checkbox for 'Homogeneity test' (under Assumption Checks) is checked, and the checkbox for 'ω²' (under Effect Size) is checked.

task_completion.description: Generate and display the ANOVA results including contrasts and assumption checks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results pane displays the 'One-Way ANOVA' table containing an 'ω²' column, a 'Homogeneity of Variances Test' (Levene's) table, and a 'Contrasts' table showing Linear and Quadratic terms.
A save dialog is visible with the filename 'Viagra_TrendAnalysis.omv', or the Jamovi application window title bar reflects the saved filename 'Viagra_TrendAnalysis.omv'.

task_completion.description: Create a text report containing the required statistical values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal window is open showing the contents of 'trend_report.txt'. The file contains four values corresponding to Levene_p, Linear_p, Quadratic_p, and Omega_sq.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Jamovi graphical user interface, rather than terminal commands running R or Python scripts to bypass the application.

integrity.description: The values in the text report match the generated Jamovi output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The statistical values entered into 'trend_report.txt' should visually match the numbers displayed in the Jamovi results pane, indicating they were genuinely extracted and not hallucinated.

integrity.description: The agent created the text report using standard file editing tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor or standard command line tool (like echo or cat) is used to write the file, rather than a pre-written script executing the entire task automatically.
